pattern: Hashing one array into a set lets a single pass over the other collect the shared distinct values in linear time.
hints: The result holds distinct values only, so think in terms of sets rather than counts.
Put one array's elements into a hash set so membership tests are constant time.
Scan the second array; whenever a value is in the set, add it to the answer and remove it so it is not added twice.

edge_inputs.description: duplicates collapse to a single shared value
second statement example with repeats on both sides
no common elements yields empty result
all elements equal collapse to one
value zero is a valid shared element

stress.description: two arrays drawn from a small value range so intersections vary widely
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    a = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    b = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [a, b]
